behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at request-construction logic boundary. A focused test can intercept the built payload/prompt and verify all taxonomy labels are present; real network or model behavior is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Call the classification function with a fixed taxonomy and stub transport, capture the exact outbound request body/prompt, and assert it contains every allowed label exactly as configured.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local post-processing/validation. A mock API response with an invalid label is sufficient to catch forwarding bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API response to return an out-of-taxonomy label (for example, "medical"). Assert module output is exactly "unknown" and not the raw label.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output stability. Mocked responses can be artificially deterministic and hide true nondeterminism/drift.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real API classifications for the same fixed document (for example, 20-100 trials under controlled prompt/model settings), then assert all returned labels are identical; flag any variance.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Real provider token accounting can differ from local estimates; mocking usage data can hide over-budget failures. Requires a real API call and real usage metadata.
behaviors.test_description: Send a representative document through the real API path with production prompt format enabled, read actual token usage reported by the provider, and assert total tokens are <= configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Need module + retry/backoff + client wiring together. Pure in-process mocks can miss parsing/handling of realistic rate-limit signals (status code/headers/error shape).
behaviors.test_description: Use a local fake API server (or HTTP-level stub) that first returns a rate-limit response (e.g., 429 with retry hint) then success. Assert module delays appropriately, retries, and finally returns successful classification instead of bubbling an error.
